macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Wall Street futures rebound as oil retreats after fresh U.S.-Iran tensions
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures edged higher as oil prices steadied, with investors balancing geopolitical risk from renewed U.S. strikes on Iran against signs the initial panic was easing. Reuters said the move helped global markets stabilize after earlier turmoil tied to the conflict.

items.title: Global equities mixed as Middle East conflict keeps markets volatile
items.summary: Reuters reported that European shares rose in choppy trade while Asian stocks also climbed, though gains were limited by a surge in oil prices and renewed inflation fears. The FTSE 100 slipped as investors weighed the regional conflict and weak corporate results.

items.title: Oil prices jump on renewed U.S. strikes and Strait of Hormuz concerns
items.summary: Reuters said oil rose after fresh U.S. strikes against Iran, reviving worries about supply disruptions and the possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. The move reinforced inflation concerns across markets and pressured bonds and risk assets.

items.title: Dollar firms and Japanese bond yields hit 30-year high on inflation fears
items.summary: Reuters reported that the U.S. dollar held firm as safe-haven demand increased and oil-driven inflation expectations rose, while the 10-year Japanese government bond yield reached a 30-year high. The market move reflected investor concern over higher prices and fiscal strain in Japan.

items.title: ECB minutes show inflation seen staying above target despite expected rate cuts
items.summary: Reuters reported that European Central Bank accounts showed policymakers were presented with projections keeping inflation above target into next year, even with nearly three expected rate hikes. The minutes suggest the ECB remains cautious about easing too quickly.

items.title: Fed officials still see need for future rate rises, says Financial Times
items.summary: The Financial Times reported that some Federal Reserve officials believed additional rate increases may still be needed to contain inflation. The comment underscores that central banks remain wary of easing financial conditions too soon.

items.title: U.S. labor market data remain soft after June jobs miss
items.summary: MarketWatch highlighted recent U.S. labor data showing the economy added 57,000 jobs in June and unemployment fell to 4.2%, alongside ADP data that pointed to slower private hiring. The numbers reinforce the view that the labor market is cooling but not collapsing.

items.title: Reuters flags major corporate and finance headlines, including OpenAI credit line and Swift blockchain launch
items.summary: Reuters reported that Bank of America extended a $520 million credit line to OpenAI and that Swift launched a blockchain-based shared ledger with banks including Citi and HSBC. Reuters also noted Citi completed an instant international dollar payment with Siam Commercial Bank.

items.title: AstraZeneca weighs on FTSE after disappointing trial results
items.summary: Reuters said the FTSE 100 fell in part because AstraZeneca pressured the index after reporting disappointing results from a late-stage trial. The stock-specific move added to broader weakness driven by geopolitical uncertainty.

items.title: IMF cuts 2026 growth outlook as Iran war fallout weighs on the world economy
items.summary: Al Jazeera reported that the IMF lowered its 2026 global growth forecast to 3 percent, citing the fallout from the Iran war and related energy shock. The IMF said AI demand may partially offset the drag from higher oil prices.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Work for workplace automation
items.summary: OpenAI unveiled ChatGPT Work, an agent designed to execute tasks across different applications and files, expanding its push into professional AI tools. The launch intensifies competition with other enterprise AI offerings.

items.title: Meta releases Muse Spark developer access and upgraded version
items.summary: Meta rolled out developer access to its Muse Spark AI model along with an upgraded version, positioning it more directly against paid AI business models from rivals such as Anthropic and OpenAI. The release is aimed at developers through API access.

items.title: OpenAI launches GPT-Live voice models
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Live, a new family of voice models that can listen and speak simultaneously in real time. The release adds to the company’s multimodal AI lineup.

items.title: xAI launches Grok 4.5
items.summary: SpaceXAI, formerly xAI, launched Grok 4.5 and described it as its most intelligent model yet, with a focus on coding and agentic tasks. The release adds to the fast-moving competition in advanced frontier models.

items.title: Anthropic adds Ben Bernanke to Long-Term Benefit Trust
items.summary: Anthropic appointed former U.S. Federal Reserve chair Ben Bernanke to its Long-Term Benefit Trust, an oversight body intended to help ensure the company stays aligned with its public mission. The move highlights growing governance scrutiny around frontier AI companies.

items.title: Meta plans AI chip manufacturing start in September
items.summary: Reuters reported that Meta plans to begin manufacturing an artificial intelligence chip in September, part of a broader effort to expand computing capacity. An internal memo said the company aims to boost overall compute to 14 gigawatts next year.

items.title: Anthropic and Google talent moves intensify AI competition
items.summary: Recent reporting highlighted major AI talent shifts, including high-profile researchers moving between Google, OpenAI, and Anthropic. The trend underscores how aggressively major labs are competing for scarce frontier-model expertise.

items.title: Mistral releases OCR 4 document-intelligence model
items.summary: Mistral reportedly launched OCR 4, a document-intelligence model supporting 170 languages with bounding boxes, block classification, and inline confidence scores. The release is positioned as a self-hosted option for enterprise document processing.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: IO Interactive to close one studio after Xbox pulls funding for unannounced project
items.summary: IO Interactive, the developer behind Hitman and 007: First Light, will shut one of its studios and lay off an unknown number of staff after Xbox withdrew funding for an upcoming project. The move is one of the clearest industry staffing stories in the latest coverage.

items.title: Sony says new PlayStation games will be digital-only after January 2028
items.summary: Sony Interactive Entertainment has clarified that production of physical discs for new PlayStation 5 games will end in January 2028. After that point, new PlayStation games will be sold only in digital format through the PlayStation Store and at retail.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass growth falls short of Microsoft’s expectations
items.summary: IGN reports that Xbox CEO Asha Sharma acknowledged Game Pass has not grown as Microsoft had expected, with subscriber totals said to be tens of millions below prior targets. The report adds to broader questions around Microsoft’s gaming subscription strategy.

items.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 1 and 2 ports are now available on PS4 and PS5
items.summary: IGN says the first two Black Ops games are now out on PlayStation 4 and PlayStation 5, though the release comes with a high price tag. The launch is one of the standout legacy-game availability stories in recent coverage.

items.title: Nintendo shuts down Mario Kart Tour after seven years
items.summary: Nintendo will discontinue the smartphone racer Mario Kart Tour this September, ending the game’s seven-year run. The shutdown is one of the most notable live-service and mobile-game developments in the latest news cycle.

items.title: Warner Bros. announces DC K.O. mobile fighting game for 2027
items.summary: Warner Bros. Interactive Entertainment and Drop Fake announced DC K.O., a new mobile fighting game for iOS and Android scheduled to launch in 2027. Pre-registration is already open.

items.title: Palworld’s 1.0 launch drives a major player resurgence
items.summary: Recent gaming coverage says Palworld’s full 1.0 release on July 10 triggered a major commercial rebound, with the game returning to the top of charts and reaching a peak of more than 961,000 concurrent Steam players. It is one of the biggest recent game-release stories by player activity.

items.title: Epic Games Store gives away Foure Tails for free
items.summary: The Epic Games Store’s current free title is Foure Tails, a story-driven card game. The giveaway is set to run until July 30.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
